intents.example_query: A partner is confused about TDEI, OS-CONNECT, OpenSidewalks, and OSM. Give a short explanation of how they relate.
An agency wants pedestrian walkway data for bus stop planning in ArcGIS. Which dataset and format should we recommend?
A user downloaded the wrong file from TDEI. How should we help them find the correct released OS-CONNECT dataset?
What does an OSW download contain, and how do edges and nodes map to sidewalks, crossings, and curb ramps?
How should I explain importing OS-CONNECT GeoJSON into ArcGIS?
A public dataset appears mislabeled or points to a wrong download. What should support staff say and do?
A user found a railroad track incorrectly marked as a walkway. How should they report it?
How long does it take for submitted corrections to appear in OS-CONNECT?
Does submitting an issue immediately change the public dataset?
An agency has many known sidewalk errors. Should they submit them individually or coordinate a structured review?
A partner asks whether this is the authoritative pedestrian dataset for their jurisdiction. What can we safely say?
A transit agency asks whether OS-CONNECT is a substitute for GTFS Pathways.
How can someone tell whether a GTFS feed includes pathways.txt?
A trip planner has poor walking directions because sidewalks lack street-name references. What should we explain?
What are street:name and is_sidepath:of:name, and when should they be used?
Which tag should we recommend for associating sidewalks with street names?
Why does sidewalk tagging matter for navigation instructions?
How should support staff explain Walksheds to a transit planner?
How is AccessMap different from Walksheds?
How can OS-CONNECT and Walksheds help with bus stop placement or access analysis?
How can OS-CONNECT support FIFA or major-event accessibility planning?
How can pedestrian accessibility data be used around schools and transit stops?
A local mapping group wants to restart sidewalk mapping. What support can TCAT offer?
What happened to the old MapWith.ai Tasking Manager, and what should we recommend now?
What beginner materials should we share with sidewalk mappers?
How should a community decide where to start mapping sidewalks?
When can volunteer-contributed sidewalk data become useful for agency planning?
When is Mappy Hours the right next step?
When should staff answer directly instead of referring someone to Mappy Hours?
Draft a professional response to an agency partner asking about OS-CONNECT correction timelines.
Rewrite this support email to be warmer but less casual and more technically precise.
Rewrite this answer so it does not overstate what we know.
Draft a reply to a partner who is reconnecting after several months about a paused sidewalk-mapping project.
This question crosses data, portal, and mapping workflows. Who should own the follow-up?
A partner has a practical use case but we do not yet know the answer. What should we say next?

patterns.title: Answer a known operational question
patterns.structure: direct answer → brief explanation → link if needed → next step

patterns.title: Answer when uncertain
patterns.structure: state what is known → state what is not confirmed → who is checking → interim guidance

patterns.title: Respond to a data error
patterns.structure: thank them → likely issue category → reporting path → review lifecycle → structured follow-up for agency-scale issues

patterns.title: Explain a tool without overselling it
patterns.structure: connect to stated use case → what it does → current limitations → next step

patterns.title: Reconnect with a prior collaborator
patterns.structure: acknowledge prior context → recognize current update → answer current question → concrete way to continue
